body.title: Add /day-close-audit Step for capability_map.md staleness
body.result: Added Step 18.6 (Capability map drift check) to ~/.claude/skills/day-close-audit/SKILL.md + Cap-map drift line in findings format. >7 day staleness triggers re-verify prompt.
body.description:
lab/docs/capability_map.md is the 'what's built today vs planned vs broken' doc. It rots fast (claims about gates, in_progress tickets, and known bugs are time-sensitive). Without a drift check it becomes wrong quietly.

DONE 2026-04-27: Added Step 18.6 (Capability map drift check) to ~/.claude/skills/day-close-audit/SKILL.md. The step computes file age in days; warns and prompts re-verification if >7 days. Updated 'Steps 1-18' wording to 'Steps 1-18.6' and added 'Cap-map drift' line to the findings report format.

Affected files:
- ~/.claude/skills/day-close-audit/SKILL.md (Step 18.6 inserted, two minor wording fixes).

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: Step 18.6 doc lives in the SKILL.md alongside other steps — canonical for the audit procedure.
- postgres-only: not applicable — skill markdown.
- oop-first: not applicable.
- test-plan-or-why-not: manual — the step is a 5-line shell snippet computing file age; no automated test needed (the audit IS the test of the audit).

Scope boundary:
IN: Step 18.6 + report-line + Steps-1-18 wording fix.
OUT: capability_map.md content rewrites, drift detection of palace subsystem_index, automated cap-map regeneration.

Test plan:
- Manual: stat ~/TheIgors/lab/docs/capability_map.md, age <= 0 days now, Step 18.6 says fresh.
- Manual on next /day-close: Step 18.6 fires, prints age, fresh|stale verdict.
- Verified 2026-04-27.
